title: Retry schedule defaults per provider
description: Default retry_config values baked into new marketing_provider_configs. Every attempt after the first waits the specified backoff_seconds[i] before the next attempt (where i = attempt_number - 1). max_attempts determines when the delivery is dead-lettered.

meta_capi.notes: Standard exponential backoff: 1m / 5m / 30m / 2h / 12h / 24h. Meta CAPI's own retry advice matches this cadence.

google_analytics_4.notes: Shorter schedule — GA4 accepts most payloads; if 4 attempts fail, it's not going to succeed on later attempts.

gtm_server.notes: Standard schedule — caller-hosted endpoints may need extended windows during their maintenance.

linkedin_insight.notes: Longer backoff — LinkedIn's outages last 2-4h historically. Schedule matches: 2m / 10m / 1h / 4h / 24h / 48h.

custom_webhook.notes: More attempts for caller-hosted endpoints (tenant may be fixing their side). Extended tail — final attempt at 72h.
